revision.comment: 新しいページ：お富士山古墳。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = お富士山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町799 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=お富士山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]中頃 |埋葬施設 = [[長持形石棺]] |規模 = 全長125m |史跡指定 = 市指定史跡（[[2001年]]） |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]] |被葬者 = 不明 |画像 = [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px]]
全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|橋梁より全景
左が前方部、右が後円部。]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|後円部墳頂
富士神社と石棺（右奥）]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[前方後円墳]]。 [[5世紀]]中頃に造られたとされる。畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]の北西部に位置する古墳で、伊勢崎市では最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座しており、「お富士山」の古墳名はこの神社に由来する。 築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設には、[[畿内]]の5世紀の王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）4月12日に伊勢崎市の史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）3月23日に群馬県の重要文化財に指定された。 == 規模 == 規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築造で、葺石が巡り、大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また、周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらも含めると古墳の長さは192メートルに及ぶ石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|200px|right|出土した[[長持形石棺]]]] 本古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部の富士神社脇に保存されている。砂岩製の石棺で、全長285センチメートル、幅121センチメートル、重さ6.8トン。石棺は6枚の石からなる。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のもので、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみ、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺については、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。なお、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定重要文化財 === * お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺 - 平成13年3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定史跡 === * お富士山古墳 - 昭和41年4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。当古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama-kofun}} * [http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ） * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳]（群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団） {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|橋梁より全景
左が前方部、右が後円部。]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|後円部墳頂
富士神社と石棺（右奥）]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が出土したことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]の北西部に位置する古墳で、伊勢崎市では最大の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設のうちで、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 規模 == 規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が巡らされ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|200px|right|出土した[[長持形石棺]]]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|石棺の複製
{{Smaller|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] 本古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のもので、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみ、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定重要文化財 === * お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺 - 平成13年3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定史跡 === * お富士山古墳 - 昭和41年4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。当古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama-kofun}} * [http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ） * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳]（群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団） {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

[[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]や群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = お富士山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町799 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=お富士山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]中頃 |埋葬施設 = [[長持形石棺]] |規模 = 墳丘長125m |史跡指定 = 伊勢崎市指定史跡「お富士山古墳」 |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]] |被葬者 = 不明 |画像 = [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 規模・構造 == 規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]や群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 平成13年3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 昭和41年4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama-kofun}} * [http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = お富士山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町799 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=お富士山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中頃 |埋葬施設 = [[長持形石棺]] |規模 = 墳丘長125m |史跡 = 伊勢崎市指定史跡「お富士山古墳」 |有形文化財 = 長持形石棺（群馬県指定文化財） |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]] |被葬者 = 不明 |画像 = [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、出土長持形石棺は群馬県指定重要文化財に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]や群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama-kofun}} *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、出土長持形石棺は群馬県指定重要文化財に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]や群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 ファイル:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|伊勢崎市赤堀歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、出土長持形石棺は群馬県指定重要文化財に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三之分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 ファイル:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|伊勢崎市赤堀歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = お富士山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町799 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=お富士山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中頃 |埋葬施設 = [[長持形石棺]] |規模 = 墳丘長125m |史跡 = 伊勢崎市指定史跡「お富士山古墳」 |有形文化財 = 長持形石棺（群馬県指定文化財） |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]] |被葬者 = 不明 |画像 = [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、出土長持形石棺は群馬県指定重要文化財に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三ノ分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 ファイル:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|伊勢崎市赤堀歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * **Anterior section** * Width: 83.2 meters * Height: 5.5 meters * Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * **Posterior section** * Diameter: 77.2 meters * Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == ### Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ### Other Artifacts Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == ### Gunma Prefecture * **Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. ### Isesaki City * **Historic Site** * Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno]] * [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ### Other Artifacts Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == ### Gunma Prefecture * **Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. ### Isesaki City * **Historic Site** * Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno]] * [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

{{Infobox Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * **Anterior section** * Width: 83.2 meters * Height: 5.5 meters * Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * **Posterior section** * Diameter: 77.2 meters * Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == ### Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ### Other Artifacts Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == ### Gunma Prefecture * **Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. ### Isesaki City * **Historic Site** * Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno]] * [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:new pages]]お富士山古墳]]

In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ### Other Artifacts Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

{{Infobox Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == ### Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ### Other Artifacts Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == ### Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ### Other Artifacts Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |shape=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound|Keyhole-shaped]] |construction_period=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |cultural_property=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property) |artifacts=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. Artifacts suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was designated a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property in 2001. == Structure == The dimensions of the kofun are as followsDimensions based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == Artifacts == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important Cultural Property)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base formed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other Artifacts === Artifacts include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofujiyama Kofun}} It has been designated as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important Cultural Property.
