A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods

{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]] {{citation needed|Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]] {{citation needed|Ofujiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods

A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. {{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Ofujiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Ofujiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

revision.comment: 17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:お富士山古墳 up to rev 101597472
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = お富士山古墳 |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町799 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=お富士山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中頃 |埋葬施設 = [[長持形石棺]] |規模 = 墳丘長125m |史跡 = 伊勢崎市指定史跡「お富士山古墳」 |有形文化財 = 長持形石棺（群馬県指定文化財） |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]] |被葬者 = 不明 |画像 = [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、出土長持形石棺は群馬県指定重要文化財に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三ノ分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 ファイル:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|伊勢崎市赤堀歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

全景
（右前が前方部、左奥が後円部。手前は[[両毛線]]） }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|橋梁より全景}}{{small|左が前方部、右が後円部。}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|富士神社と石棺（右奥）。}}]] '''お富士山古墳'''（おふじやまこふん、御富士山古墳）は、[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]安堀町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、出土長持形石棺は群馬県指定重要文化財に指定されている。 [[5世紀]]中頃の築造とされ、畿内王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されたことで知られる。 == 概要 == [[伊勢崎市]]北西部に位置し、伊勢崎市では最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である。古墳は前方部を西南西に向けるが、前方部の一部は[[両毛線]]敷設のため削られている。後円部墳頂には富士神社（富士浅間神社）が鎮座し、「お富士山」の古墳名は同神社に由来する。 古墳の築造時期は、出土遺物から[[5世紀]]中頃と推定されている。埋葬施設として、5世紀の[[畿内]]王墓特有の[[長持形石棺]]が使用されている。 古墳は[[昭和]]41年（[[1966年]]）に伊勢崎市指定史跡に指定され、長持形石棺は[[平成]]13年（[[2001年]]）に群馬県指定重要文化財に指定された。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り規模の数値は現地説明板による。。 * 墳丘長：125メートル * 前方部 - [[両毛線]]建設のため、一部削られている。 ** 幅：83.2メートル ** 高さ：5.5メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：77.2メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル 主軸は北74度東現地説明板。。墳丘は3段築成で、葺石が葺かれ、さらに大型円筒埴輪が並べられていた。また周囲には盾形の周堀が巡らされており、それらを含めると古墳の総長は192メートルにもなる石棺保存施設の説明書。。 == 出土品 == === 長持形石棺 === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|[[長持形石棺]]（群馬県指定文化財）}}]] [[ファイル:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|right|200px|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{Small|[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示。}}]] お富士山古墳に使用された[[長持形石棺]]は、後円部墳頂の富士神社脇に保存されている。石棺は砂岩製の6枚の石板から成り、全長は285センチメートル、幅は121センチメートル、重さは6.8トンである。箱形は底石（そこいし）・側石（がわいし）2枚・小口石（こぐちいし）2枚で形成され、その上にかまぼこ形の蓋石（ふたいし）が載せられる。 この形状の石棺は5世紀における[[畿内]]の王墓特有のものになり、この石棺の使用例は群馬県内では[[太田天神山古墳]]（群馬県[[太田市]]）のみで、[[関東地方]]でも数例しか見つかっていないその他の関東地方における長持形石棺の使用例には、[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（[[千葉県]][[木更津市]]）と[[三ノ分目大塚山古墳]]（千葉県[[香取市]]）が指摘されるが、定かではない。。石棺の形態・技法は畿内のものと同様であるため、畿内の工人が当地で製作したと考えられており、畿内との強いつながりが指摘される。 本石棺は、[[江戸時代]]の『発墳暦』や『伊勢崎風土記』にも記録されている。また、この石棺を型取りして複製したものは群馬県立歴史博物館でも展示されている。 === その他 === その他の出土品としては、滑石による刀子の模造品がある。 ファイル:お富士山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|伊勢崎市赤堀歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）3月23日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1354006355686/index.html お富士山古墳所在長持形石棺]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 === 伊勢崎市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** お富士山古墳 - 1966年（昭和41年）4月12日指定[http://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/www/contents/1355363671957/index.html お富士山古墳]（伊勢崎市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 現地説明板（伊勢崎市教育委員会、平成11年2月5日設置）、石棺保存施設の説明書 == 関連項目 == * [[毛野]] * [[太田天神山古墳]] - 東日本最大の古墳。お富士山古墳と同じく長持形石棺が使用された。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html お富士山古墳] - 伊勢崎市ホームページ * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html お富士山古墳] - 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 {{DEFAULTSORT:おふしやまこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical Haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|お富士山古墳|version=101597472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]]

Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|お富士山古墳|version=101597472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]] [[Category:伊勢崎市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Isesaki, Gunma
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical Haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|お富士山古墳|version=101597472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]] [[Category:History of Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical Haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|お富士山古墳|version=101597472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]] [[Category:History of Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]] {{Commons category|Ofujiyama Kofun}}

It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical Haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Ofujiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] |coordinates={{coord|36|20|16.93|N|139|10|19.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ofujiyama Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Mid-5th century |burial=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus |length=125 meters |heritage=Isesaki City Designated Historic Site |notes=Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1) |burial goods=[[Cylindrical Haniwa|Cylindrical haniwa]] |image=[[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-2.JPG|300px]]
Panoramic view
{{small|Right: anterior section; left: posterior section. Foreground: Ryōmō Line tracks.}} }} [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun zenkei-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Panoramic view from the bridge}}{{small|Left: anterior section; right: posterior section.}}]] [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun funcho.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Posterior section}}{{small|Fujisan Shrine and sarcophagus (far right).}}]] '''Ofujiyama Kofun''' (お富士山古墳), also referred to as御富士山古墳, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[:en:Isesaki, Gunma|Isesaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Isesaki City, and its Nagamochi-style sarcophagus is recognized as a Prefectural Important designation1. The mound, built during the mid-5th century, is notable for its use of a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a characteristic feature of royal tombs in the Kinki region. == Overview == Situated in the northwestern part of Isesaki City, Ofujiyama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in the city. The anterior section faces west-southwest, but part of it was removed to accommodate the construction of the [[Ryōmō Line]]. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Isesaki City Akabori History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Gunma Prefecture === * '''Important designation1 (Tangible designation1)''' ''' Nagamochi-style sarcophagus from Ofujiyama Kofun (archaeological material) – Designated March 23, 2001. === Isesaki City === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Ofujiyama Kofun – Designated April 12, 1966. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board at the Site (Isesaki City Board of Education, installed February 5, 1999) * Sarcophagus Preservation Facility Documentation == Related Sites == * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno]] * [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] – The largest kofun in eastern Japan, also featuring a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ofujiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.isesaki.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/shitei/shiseki/3380.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Isesaki City Website) * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/ohujiyama.html Ofujiyama Kofun] (Gunma Archaeological Research Center) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|お富士山古墳|version=101597472|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ofujiyama Kofun]] [[ja:お富士山古墳]] [[Category:History of Isesaki, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定史跡]] {{Commons category|Ofujiyama Kofun}}

It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. A Fujisan Shrine (dedicated to the deity of Mount Fuji) sits atop the posterior section, from which the kofun derives its name. burial goods suggest the mound was constructed in the mid-5th century. Its burial facility features a Nagamochi-style sarcophagus, a hallmark of royal tombs from the Kinki region during the same period. The site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in 1966 and the sarcophagus was classified as a Prefectural Important designation1 in 2001. == burial == The area of the kofun are as followsarea based on the explanation board at the site.: * Total length: 125 meters * '''Anterior section''' ''' Width: 83.2 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters ''' Some sections removed for the Ryōmō Line construction. * '''Posterior section''' ''' Diameter: 77.2 meters ''' Height: 9.5 meters The mound's orientation is approximately 74° east of north. It features a three-tiered structure covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and lined with large cylindrical haniwa. Surrounding the mound is a shield-shaped moat, which extends the total length of the site to 192 meters. == burial goods == === Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus === [[File:Ofujiyama-kofun sekkan.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus (Prefectural Important designation1)}}]] [[File:お富士山古墳石棺（復元）.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Replica of the sarcophagus}}{{small|Displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History.}}]] The [[Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus]] from Ofujiyama Kofun is preserved near the shrine at the posterior section. It is composed of six sandstone slabs: * Total length: 285 cm * Width: 121 cm * Weight: 6.8 tons The sarcophagus features a rectangular base typeed by a floor slab, two side slabs, and two end slabs. A rounded, domed lid rests on top. This sarcophagus type is characteristic of royal tombs in the Kinki region during the 5th century. In Gunma Prefecture, only the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|Ota Tenjinyama Kofun]] shares this feature. Few examples are found elsewhere in the Kanto regionOther examples include Takayanagi Chōshizuka Kofun and San-no-Funeme Ōtsukayama Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.. The craftsmanship and materials suggest that artisans from the Kinki region constructed the sarcophagus locally, underscoring strong ties with the Kinki region. Historical records such as the Edo-period "Hatsuhunreki" and "Isesaki Fudoki" also document the sarcophagus. A replica is displayed at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. === Other burial goods === burial goods include imitation stone knives made from steatite. File:お富士山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
